plan_metrics.plan_content:
Here's a summary of what I found and the plan I've written:

---

## What Already Exists

- **`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`** â€” a solid external-store hook, but it only handles `"light" | "dark" | "system"` by toggling a `.dark` class. No named themes, no provider.
- **`apps/web/src/index.css`** â€” Tailwind v4 with CSS custom properties (`--background`, `--foreground`, `--primary`, etc.). Dark mode is the `@variant dark` block inside `:root`. No per-theme overrides.
- **`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** â€” Settings page already has an Appearance section with a 3-option radio group that consumes `useTheme()`. This section gets replaced by the new `ThemeSelector`.

---

## Plan Overview (8 steps)

| # | What | Where |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | New file: `ThemeId`, `ThemeColors`, `ThemeDefinition`, `BUILT_IN_THEMES` | `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` |
| 2 | Add `./theme` subpath export | `packages/shared/package.json` |
| 3 | New file: `ThemeProvider` component + `useTheme` hook (Context-based, localStorage + flash prevention) | `apps/web/src/providers/ThemeProvider.tsx` |
| 4 | Replace existing hook with a one-line re-export | `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` |
| 5 | Wrap app tree with `ThemeProvider` | `apps/web/src/router.ts` |
| 6 | Add `--t3-sidebar/editor/terminal/status-bar` vars to `:root`; add `[data-theme="t3-midnight"]` override block | `apps/web/src/index.css` |
| 7 | New file: `ThemeSelector` swatch grid (3 cards with mini layout previews using inline `style` from theme color data) | `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` |
| 8 | Replace radio buttons in Settings Appearance section with `<ThemeSelector />` | `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` |

**Key design choices:**
- `t3-dark` and `t3-light` require zero new CSS â€” they map directly to the existing `:root` light and dark variables
- `t3-midnight` is a dark variant: it inherits `.dark` class styles and only a single `[data-theme="t3-midnight"]` block overrides the few tokens that differ (bluer/darker palette + purple primary)
- No new dependencies â€” uses `React.createContext`, `useState`, `useEffect` already present
